Additional Info
Success Story
On the first day of implementation, Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) cameras immediately
demonstrated their value to the El Cajon Police Department with two successful apprehensions.
On July 28, 2023, a real-time alert notified officers that a vehicle stolen during a violent robbery and carjacking in
Lemon Grove on July 22 had been detected by the ALPR system. Through rapid coordination between agencies, a

--- page 7 ---
San Diego County Sheriff�s deputy located the vehicle within minutes and safely apprehended two suspects.
Later that evening, another ALPR alert identified a vehicle that had been stolen during a commercial burglary in
Winterhaven, California (Imperial County) on July 26. An El Cajon Police officer quickly located the occupied vehicle
in a gas station parking lot and arrested the suspect without incident.
These two incidents on day one highlight how real-time ALPR technology can quickly connect crimes across
jurisdictions and help officers safely and efficiently locate stolen vehicles and suspects.
Success Story
On October 5, 2024, at approximately 9:15 PM, officers from the El Cajon Police Department responded to a reported
stabbing in the 800 block of Benedict Avenue in El Cajon, California.Upon arrival, officers encountered a suspect
armed with a knife. When officers attempted to detain him, the suspect fled on foot and attempted to carjack a passing
motorist, who was able to escape. Officers continued searching the area and ultimately apprehended the suspect. He
was taken into custody and booked into the San Diego County Sheriff's Office Central Jail.
The victim, a 71-year-old male, was found with a stab wound to his torso and transported to a nearby hospital. On
October 12, the victim died from his injuries.
During the investigation, detectives determined that the victim and suspectboth homelesshad recently met, and the
victim had been giving the suspect a ride when their vehicle broke down. During the suspect�s attempt to flee the
scene, Flock Safety automated license plate reader cameras helped investigators identify the motorist the suspect
attempted to carjack, providing critical witness information that supported the homicide investigation.
Success Story
Over the course of one month, a suspect committed at least eleven thefts at Food 4 Less, repeatedly entering the
store, concealing groceries and health products, and leaving without paying. The total reported loss exceeded
$4,000.
A detective with the El Cajon Police Department developed a key lead when the suspect vehicle�s license plate was
identified and linked to surveillance footage from the store. The investigation led to the identification of a 39-year-old
resident of unincorporated El Cajon who was also found to be selling the stolen merchandise. The vehicle�s license
plate was entered into the city�s Flock Safety automated license plate reader system. On May 9, the system
generated an alert when the vehicle was detected traveling southbound on North Mollison Avenue. A patrol officer
quickly located and stopped the vehicle, confirmed the suspect as the driver, and took him into custody. He was later
booked into the San Diego County Sheriff's Office Central Jail on charges including organized retail theft, grand theft,
and conspiracy.
Success Story
On February 29, 2024, at approximately 4:14 PM, the El Cajon Police Department responded to reports of gunfire at
a dental office on North Magnolia Avenue in El Cajon, California. Officers arrived within minutes and located three
victims suffering from gunshot wounds. Despite lifesaving efforts, one adult male died at the scene, while two
additional victims were transported to the hospital in serious but stable condition.
Witnesses reported that the suspect, a 29-year-old local resident and former patient of the dental office, fled in a
white U-Haul pickup truck. Officers quickly utilized the city�s Flock Safety automated license plate reader system,
which captured an image of the vehicle and its license plate. Investigators rapidly identified the rental vehicle and
confirmed it had been rented in San Diego.
Because this information was obtained so quickly, detectives were able to distribute the suspect vehicle�s photo and
license plate to the public through news media and department social media, which resulted in his arrest within hours
after his murderous rampage.
Success Story
On October 28, 2023, at approximately 11:57 PM, the El Cajon Police Department responded to multiple 9-1-1 calls
reporting a large house party and gunshots in the 700 block of Mahogany Drive in El Cajon, California. Officers
arrived to find numerous individuals fleeing the area and located two victimsa 16-year-old male and an 18-year-old
malesuffering from critical gunshot wounds. Both victims later died after being transported to local trauma hospitals.

--- page 8 ---
Homicide detectives determined that at least two suspects fled the scene in a vehicle prior to police arrival.
Investigators used the city�s Flock Safety automated license plate reader cameras to identify the suspected getaway
vehicle, which led to the identification of two suspects from San Diego.
One suspect was later taken into custody by the Chula Vista Police Department on November 18 during an unrelated
firearm arrest. On November 20, officers and detectives from the El Cajon Police Department, with assistance from
the United States Marshals Service Fugitive Task Force and the East County Regional Gang Task Force, located and
arrested the second suspect in San Diego, bringing the homicide investigation closer to resolution.
